steps.title: Identify two points on line j from the graph
steps.content: The graph shows two marked points on line $j$: one at $(0, 5)$ and one at $(1, 9)$.

steps.title: Calculate the slope of line j using the slope formula
steps.content:
Using the slope formula $m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$:
$$m = \frac{9 - 5}{1 - 0} = \frac{4}{1} = 4$$

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines property
steps.content: Parallel lines have equal slopes. Since line $k$ is parallel to line $j$, line $k$ has the same slope as line $j$. That matches choice D — The answer is 4.
